identity.canonical_title: Time as a Toroidal Winding Parameter

theory.canonical_statement: Chapter: Time as a Toroidal Winding Parameter: Phenomenology, Retrocausality, and the Hopf Fibre Primary Registry IDs: 007 (temporal displacement factor — time flows differently near $p 0$), 011 (causal loop invariant — quantised retrocausal interactions) Secondary IDs: 003 (dimensional ambiguity — the temporal dimension participates), 000 (TZP as fixed point — the attractor that retrocausal paths converge to) Tertiary IDs: 1121–1135 (bio quantum interface — the nervous system as TZP sensor) The temporal displacement factor (ID 007: $\alpha(t) = dt'/dt$) is the formal expression of the subjective time distortion described in the chapter — "time felt to stop, then accelerate strangely."
theory.technical_interpretation: ID 011 The causal loop invariant (ID 011: $I_\text{CL} = \oint_\Gamma A\cdot dx$) quantises the interaction between causal loops and the TZP. The 1999 experience involves what appears to be a closed causal loop: an event at location 1 (the tent interior) simultaneously with an event at location 2 (the exterior), without an apparent classical causal signal connecting them. In the TZP framework, this simultaneity is explained by both events being on the same Hopf fibre — a great circle of $S^3$ that projects to a torus in $\mathbb{R}^3$. The causal loop $\Gamma$ encircling both events has: The quantisation means the events can be simultaneous only when the loop integral is an integer multiple of $u_T$ — providing a selection rule that limits which pairs of events can be "linked" by a Hopf fibre. **Not all pairs of simultaneous events are linked; only those satisfying the quantisation condi In technical terms, the entry packages the selected formula as the operative carrier, the nearby equations as the progression layer, and the symbol key as the compact interpretation of variables used by the source.

It identifies the mathematical spine of the cited chapter and gives the entry a concrete equation, symbol key, and derivation chain for the four-page production layout.

The entry should be read as part of the larger Opera Omnia progression: a local equation is selected, its surrounding prose supplies the physical or mathematical role, and the production packet keeps that source connection visible.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The equation progression records I_\text{CL} = \oint_\Gamma A\cdot dx = n\cdot u_T \quad (n \in \mathbb{Z}); \alpha(t) = dt'/dt; I_\text{CL} = \oint_\Gamma A\cdot dx; \det g = 0. These relations give the support page enough structure to show how the canonical formula arises from nearby source material rather than from a disconnected title alone.
